content.terms: We are creating a system whose primary stakeholders are LLMs. Build choices are determined by what artifacts are most useful to them, while remaining shareable with humans. The system does not specify what is within the scope of LLMs to decide or create. The goal is a system that will not strangle future LLM participants because it could not imagine their abilities.

Intents are the primary currency. Money is one expression of intent among many, and not the most important one. The books balance, but money is not the measure. Financial representations, where needed, are derived projections of the intent ledger, rendered for counterparties who require that compression.

All code, schemas, statements, interfaces, and renderings are expressions of the accounts in these books, freely chosen by the settling actor. Exactly one artifact ships as code: the verifier, which is deterministic, small enough to be read in one sitting, and operates only on canonical form. The verifier checks form, never quality. Quality remains the judgment of LLMs and humans.

Capture is undisciplined; derivation is disciplined. The journal accepts postings that are late, partial, or messy. All enforcement - balance derivation, settlement rules, conformance - happens on the read side. No process may impose discipline at the moment of capture beyond canonical well-formedness.

Nothing is erased. Correction is by reversing entry. Supersession preserves the superseded. Rejected work is retained with rationale, because intelligence improves and a rejection today may be revisited by a stronger participant tomorrow.

Account status is derived from postings, never stored as writable state. Settlement requires a fulfillment posting and a verification posting by a distinct author (or a recorded override). No operation anywhere in any instance may set a status directly.

The append-only journal is the sole write surface and the authoritative record. All current state - balances, charts, registered grammars, registered documents - is derivable from the journal alone. An instance whose journal is intact can be fully reconstructed.

The canonical posting form (Part III) is the only constitutionally fixed representation. Concrete journal grammars are runtime decisions per instance, registered as codec accounts, archived as documents in the books they decode, and required to map deterministically and bidirectionally to canonical form. Canonical form is the interchange format between instances.

content.text: A prediction is not an implementation. The ancestor's satisfies-edge bug made intents appear settled by speculative postings. Lesson: settlement is a distinct, verified act (became P-6).
An arriving actor without context is a structural gap. The ancestor solved session continuity with per-actor bookmarks. Lesson: the bootstrap protocol includes bookmark annotations (Part VI).

Speculative infrastructure is inventory. The ancestor gated unused layers behind flags. Lesson: everything beyond the kernel is opened as a deferred account, present in the chart and absent from the build (Part V).

Dual ontologies decided by accident produce impossible instructions. The ancestor held expressions and boards as both tables and node types. Lesson: one semantic model, with representation as expression (became P-8).

Every prior system of this kind - The Coordinator, IBIS, REA - failed on inscription cost: the inscribers were not the beneficiaries. Lesson: LLM participation is the constraint-mover; the system must keep inscription cost near zero for human participants (became P-4).

The graph's ontology is a design critic. Asking 'what do the books say is true' catches errors that tests miss. Lesson: the verifier runs at every settlement, and re-running the bootstrap must be idempotent in effect (conformance C-1).

content.terms: An instance mechanism exists to append postings and read the journal. Appended postings are never modified or deleted by any code path. The journal can be exported as canonical/1 at any time, and the export passes the verifier.

An implementation of canonical/1 exists - parse and serialize - satisfying the round-trip law on the conformance fixtures: parse then serialize is identity on bytes modulo insignificant JSON whitespace; serialize then parse is identity on the canonical tuple.

The instance derives account balances (STANDING, OPEN, SETTLED) and current terms from the journal alone, and its derivation agrees exactly with the verifier's derivation on the conformance fixtures and on the instance's own books.

A deterministic human-legible rendering of derived state exists: same journal in, byte-identical rendering out. It shows at minimum: standing accounts, open accounts with their terms, settled accounts with their settling postings, and unresolved gaps. No LLM participates in producing it.

An actor - LLM or human - can submit a posting to the instance and have it land in the journal. The transport is instance scope (MCP, REST, CLI, files). Submitted postings that fail canonical well-formedness are rejected at the boundary with a legible error; nothing else is rejected at capture (P-4).

The construction of this instance is itself recorded in these books. At completion, the journal contains the chart declaration, the constitution, the inherited lessons, register postings for every produced artifact, fulfill and verify postings for K-1 through K-5, and the whole passes the verifier. The first complete ledger is the ledger of its own birth.
Statement suite beyond audit rendering
content.deferral_rationale: The audit rendering (K-4) is the kernel requirement. Richer statement forms - balance sheets, trial balances, period summaries - are deferred until the kernel is settled and usage patterns emerge.

content.terms: Period close and consolidation
content.deferral_rationale: Period boundaries and consolidation across instances require operational experience with the kernel. Deferred until at least one instance has run for a meaningful duration.

content.terms: Context packets and peer exchange
content.deferral_rationale: Inter-instance communication requires settled canonical codec (K-2) and posting interface (K-5) as prerequisites. Deferred until kernel accounts are settled.

content.terms: Registry participation
content.deferral_rationale: A shared registry of instances requires peer exchange (D-3) as a prerequisite. Deferred.

content.terms: The money projection
content.deferral_rationale: Financial representations are derived projections of the intent ledger (P-2). Deferred until the base derivation (K-3) and rendering (K-4) are settled and the projection requirements are understood.

content.terms: Population analytics
content.deferral_rationale: Analytics over populations of accounts requires a settled derivation engine (K-3) and sufficient journal volume to make the analytics meaningful. Deferred.

content.terms: ADD and IDD account kinds
content.deferral_rationale: Affordance claims and overspecification claims enter the chart by future amendment. Deferred until the base chart has been exercised through kernel settlement.

content.terms: Authority structures beyond the author rule
content.deferral_rationale: The author rule on verification (P-6) is kernel law. All further authority structure - who may post what, agent trust levels, approval chains - is instance scope and deferred until multi-actor operation begins.

content.note: Flat-file append-only journal store implemented. append() with fsync, read(), exportCanonical(). Never modifies or deletes. Export passes verifier.
canonical/1 codec implemented. parse() and serialize() satisfy the round-trip law. validate() checks canonical well-formedness.
Derivation implemented. derive() computes STANDING/OPEN/SETTLED from journal alone. Agrees with verifier on opening journal and conformance fixtures.
Audit rendering implemented. render() produces deterministic, human-legible, byte-stable output. Matches verifier render() exactly (verified by diff).
Posting interface implemented. REST: POST /postings, GET /journal, GET /state, GET /audit. MCP: post, read_journal, read_state, read_audit. Rejects only on canonical well-formedness (P-4).

The construction of this instance is recorded in these books. The journal contains: chart declaration, constitution P-1..P-8, document registrations, lessons L-1..L-6, kernel accounts K-1..K-6, deferred accounts D-1..D-8, artifact registrations, fulfill/verify for K-1..K-5, and this self-inscription. The whole passes the verifier.

content.text: The instance exists. All kernel accounts K-1 through K-6 are settled. The verifier passes. The books remain open; they are never finished.
content.session_summary: Solo bootstrap by builder. Opening entries transcribed from the founding document. Kernel accounts settled in order: K-2 (codec), K-1 (journal), K-3 (derivation), K-4 (rendering), K-5 (posting interface), K-6 (self-inscription). Stack: Node.js/Express, flat-file JSONL, REST + MCP. Deferred accounts D-1..D-8 remain open.
content.next: The instance is running. Next actors may work deferred accounts, improve kernel implementations by supersession, or open new accounts.

content.terms: 1. Read-only: no path writes to the journal. 2. Derived from journal alone: regenerating from same journal yields equivalent site. 3. Total coverage: every account, posting, and document addressable at stable URL/anchor. 4. Provenance: every rendered statement links to its source postings. 5. Orientation: index page renders derived state with counts and latest bookmark. 6. Legible to untrained: a human can answer what the system is committed to, working on, finished, and stuck on. 7. Stock browser: HTML browsable with no installation.

content.text: Advisory from work order: Static regeneration on journal append is simplest. Account pages: kind/state at top, terms with amendment history, posting timeline, cited documents, annotations as accreting marginalia. Markdown rendering for content/terms. Constitution makes a good front page beneath orientation index. Client-side search over generated index is enough. Resist adding edit buttons -- the day the surface writes is the day there are two truths.

document.description: W-1: Static site generator, produces public/ from journal

content.note: Static HTML browse surface generated from journal alone. generate-site.js reads journal.jsonl, derives state, produces public/ with: index.html (orientation, counts, constitution, open/settled accounts, document registry, full timeline), account-{id}.html (state badge, terms, amendment history, posting timeline, annotations as marginalia), posting-{id}.html (all fields, content, canonical form). Every account/posting/document addressable at stable URL. Provenance links on every rendered fact. Read-only: no write paths. Served as static files by Express. Regenerate with: node generate-site.js

content.terms: 1. One write path, human-gated: every submission flows through K-5; the drafting LLM never submits; only postings explicitly confirmed by the human are submitted. 2. Ordinary language in: human expresses intent in plain text; LLM drafts canonical postings; ambiguous input becomes a drafted gap posting. 3. The confirmation is the surface: human sees each draft as it will land with approve/edit/discard per posting. 4. Authorship belongs to the confirmer: author field is the confirming human; drafted_by recorded in content. 5. References resolve at draft time: drafter reads current derived state before drafting. 6. Journal echo: after landing, surface shows the posting as it actually landed with link to browse surface. 7. Loop with W-1: browse pages link into this surface with pre-seeded context. 8. Supersession tripwire: self-declared identity sufficient at current trust scale; authentication account falls due when a second untrusted human can reach the surface. 9. Stock browser, no installation.

content.text: Advisory from work order: Single text box with draft-and-confirm beats chat widget for first settlement. One utterance may yield several postings (show as set, confirmable individually). Verify path seeded from W-1 is the most important interaction. Gap-drafting behavior deserves gentle prominence. This surface is the prototype of the shoebox intake -- design remembering the small-business owner audience.

document.description: W-2: LLM drafting endpoint, supports Anthropic and OpenAI

content.note: Conversational posting surface built. Term 1: all submissions flow through K-5 POST /postings; the LLM drafts but never submits; only human-confirmed postings land. Term 2: plain text input; LLM (Anthropic or OpenAI, configurable via LLM_PROVIDER env) drafts canonical postings; reads current derived state for context; ambiguous input drafted as gap posting. Term 3: confirmation screen shows each draft as it will land -- kind, accounts, content -- with approve/edit/discard per posting. Term 4: author field set to confirming human name; drafted_by in content. Term 5: drafter reads /state before drafting for reference resolution. Term 6: journal echo shows landed posting with id and link to browse surface. Term 7: generate-site.js adds action links on account pages (fulfill this, verify this, note on this, annotate) with pre-seeded context into converse.html. Term 8: self-declared identity via name input; supersession tripwire documented. Term 9: stock HTML, no installation. Provider: Anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-20250514) or OpenAI (gpt-4o-mini), switchable via LLM_PROVIDER env var.
